commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7a3f6602fa9bd7d5d6acac978185d7261166c928)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes recursive display-change recovery scheduling in `MenuBarVisibilityWatcher` while keeping one post-recovery status-item recreation and adding a comment about stopping further retries.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review; the PR body provides a concrete Ice/display-change plan, but it is unchecked. The guidance regression is source-reproducible from the diff because the deleted branch was the only display-change path that called `presentGuidance`.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor runtime proof and a code change preserving guidance; ClawSweeper repair automation should not be requested while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix: stop retrying display-change recovery to avoid corrupting menu bar managers
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove the recursive `schedulePostScreenChangeRecoveryVerification` retry loop from `verifyScreenChangeRecoveryIfNeeded`
- One recreation attempt is sufficient; further retries can't distinguish a genuine macOS block from a menu bar manager's transient re-registration state

## Problem

The retry loop introduced in c305858c called `recreateStatusItemsForVisibilityRecovery()` up to four times per display event. After each recreation, a menu bar manager can temporarily leave the new `NSStatusItem` without a window while re-registering it — making it appear blocked and triggering another recreation cycle. Each cycle is visible to Control Center and menu bar managers as a brand-new item, corrupting Control Center layout and causing managers to re-expose the previously hidden icon.

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `./Scripts/compile_and_run.sh`
- [ ] Hide CodexBar via Ice or another menu bar manager
- [ ] Trigger a display change (System Settings → Displays → change resolution and revert)
- [ ] Confirm Control Center is not corrupted and the manager does not re-expose CodexBar
- [ ] Stream logs and confirm `"recreating status items again"` appears at most once per display event: `log stream --process CodexBar --level debug | grep -i "recreating status items"`
target.author: diazdesandi
